chapter: JEF P104 – A Question of Trust
title: A Question of Trust – Medium SAQs

questions.question: Explain why Horace Danby is described as respectable but dishonest.
questions.answer: Horace appears respectable because he is a successful locksmith and law-abiding citizen, but he is dishonest as he secretly steals once every year.

questions.question: Why did Horace Danby steal only from wealthy people?
questions.answer: Horace believed stealing from rich people was less harmful and justified his crime as he did not steal for survival.

questions.question: Describe Horace Danby’s method of planning a robbery.
questions.answer: Horace studied the house carefully, observed the servants’ movements, examined the wiring, and prepared his tools in advance.

questions.question: How did the absence of servants help Horace?
questions.answer: The servants’ absence made Horace confident that he could complete the robbery without interruption.

questions.question: Why did Horace trust the young lady?
questions.answer: He trusted her because she behaved calmly, confidently, and spoke like the owner of the house.

questions.question: Explain the irony in Horace Danby’s profession.
questions.answer: Horace makes locks to protect property but breaks locks while committing thefts, which creates irony.

questions.question: How did the young lady manipulate Horace?
questions.answer: She pretended to be the owner’s wife and claimed she had forgotten the safe’s combination, forcing Horace to open it.

questions.question: Why did Horace remove his gloves?
questions.answer: He removed his gloves to light the young lady’s cigarette, which led to leaving fingerprints.

questions.question: What role does Sherry play in the story?
questions.answer: Sherry creates a sense of normalcy and makes Horace feel safe inside the house.

questions.question: Why was Horace arrested despite his experience?
questions.answer: He was arrested because he made an emotional mistake and left fingerprints on the safe.

questions.question: Describe the character of the young lady.
questions.answer: The young lady is clever, confident, manipulative, and morally ruthless.

questions.question: Why does Horace fear prison?
questions.answer: He fears prison because it would separate him from his books and freedom.

questions.question: Explain the theme of trust in the story.
questions.answer: The story shows how blind trust can lead to betrayal and downfall.

questions.question: How is the title ‘A Question of Trust’ justified?
questions.answer: Horace’s misplaced trust in the young lady leads directly to his failure.

questions.question: What belief of Horace is proved wrong?
questions.answer: His belief that there is honour among thieves is proved wrong.

questions.question: Explain the irony in Horace’s final job.
questions.answer: Horace becomes an assistant librarian in prison, gaining books but losing freedom.

questions.question: What does Horace’s double life indicate?
questions.answer: It shows hypocrisy and moral contradiction in his character.

questions.question: Why did Horace agree to help the young lady?
questions.answer: He agreed because he was desperate to avoid being reported to the police.

questions.question: What lesson does the story convey?
questions.answer: The story teaches that crime and trust cannot coexist.

questions.question: How does overconfidence contribute to Horace’s downfall?
questions.answer: His overconfidence prevents him from suspecting danger.

questions.question: Explain Horace’s selective morality.
questions.answer: Horace justifies stealing by believing he is not harming the poor.

questions.question: What does Horace’s sneezing symbolise?
questions.answer: It symbolises human weakness and vulnerability.

questions.question: Why does the author include humour in the story?
questions.answer: Humour softens criticism while conveying a serious moral message.

questions.question: What contrast exists between Horace and the young lady?
questions.answer: Horace has moral hesitation, while the young lady shows none.

questions.question: Why can Horace not be considered a typical thief?
questions.answer: He steals selectively, plans carefully, and believes he follows moral limits.
